title: invitations — data retention windows
description: Retention per model. Drives CleanupAcceptedInvitationsJob + the compliance module's retention orchestrator. Durations ISO-8601. Compliance tier drives DSAR + right-to-erasure behaviour per data-classes.json.

pending.description: Never soft-deleted while pending; but a pending invitation older than 180 days without any activity is a data-quality issue and gets hard-deleted.

accepted.description: Kept 90 days for admin visibility; soft-deleted then; hard-deleted at 1 year for GDPR compliance.

bounced.description: Kept longer for deliverability learning (avoid re-inviting confirmed-invalid addresses).

retention.description: GDPR erasure: on subject request, the invitation is anonymised in place (email → `erased-<hash>@invalid.local`, message + metadata purged) and cascaded to InvitationEvent rows. Full row deletion happens via the retention pipeline.

Append-only. Retained 1 year hot for SOC 2 evidence + funnel analysis. Older rows shipped to cold storage per compliance module's export orchestrator. NOT deleted on right-to-erasure — the event row is anonymised (ip_address, user_agent, city zeroed) but the invitation_id + event + occurred_at + transport are preserved for aggregate deliverability + audit.

complianceTiers.customer_data: Customer-owned records (invitations). Retained for the customer's benefit until the retention window. Subject to erasure on request.
complianceTiers.audit_log: Immutable audit records. Retained for the shortest of the compliance regime maximum + the operational SLA. Anonymised, not deleted, on erasure requests.
